message: Node started: Propose a robust algorithm to generate N non-overlapping axis-aligned rectangles within a 10000x10000 grid. Each rectangle i must contain the point (xi, yi) and attempt to approximate area ri. Consider strategies such as: 1. A sweep-line approach to partition the space into strips or cells. 2. A recursive quadtree-like subdivision. 3. A greedy approach where rectangles are expanded from their points until they hit a boundary or another rectangle. The strategy must guarantee no overlaps and that all rectangles stay within [0, 10000). Provide the logic in pseudocode or a step-by-step mathematical description.
